Version.Name: Carcinogenicity model (IRFMN-ISSCAN-CGX)
Version.Summary: QSAR classification model for Carcinogenicity (IRFMN/ISSCAN-CGX) based on the ISSCAN-CGX dataset

Guide.Description: The model provides a qualitative prediction of carcinogenicity (expert assessment based on carcinogenic effects in different species). It is implemented inside the VEGA online platform, accessible at: http://www.vega-qsar.eu/
Guide.Model: The model has been built as a set of rules, extracted with Sarpy software from a dataset obtained from the union of the ISS carcinogenicity (ISSCAN) database from Istituto Superiore della Sanità, and of the Carcinogenicity Genotoxicity eXperience (CGX) dataset. The Sarpy software has been used with a cross-validated procedure, ending with the extraction of a set of 43 rules (structural alerts) related to carcinogenic activity. These rules are expressed SMARTS representing molecular fragments. If at least one rule is matching with the given compound, a “Carcinogen” prediction is given. Otherwise, a “Possible NON-Carcinogen” prediction is given.

AD.Name: Similar_Molecules
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: strongly similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: only moderately similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: no similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found

AD.Name: Accuracy
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is good
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is not optimal
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is not adequate

AD.Name: Concordance
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that agree with the predicted value
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: some similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that disagree with the predicted value
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that disagree with the predicted value

AD.Name: ACF_SimCheck
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: all atom centered fragment of the compound have been found in the compounds of the training set
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: some atom centered fragment of the compound have not been found in the compounds of the training set or are rare fragments
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: a prominent number of atom centered fragments of the compound have not been found in the compounds of the training set or are rare fragments

AD.Name: AD_Index
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: predicted substance is into the Applicability Domain of the model
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: predicted substance could be out of the Applicability Domain of the model
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: predicted substance is out of the the Applicability Domain of the model

Guide.Structural_Alerts: Following, the list of the 127 rules for carcinogenicity, expressed as SMARTS strings: SA 1: O=NNCC SA 2: c1occc1 SA 3: O=CN(N)C SA 4: CCCN(CC)CC SA 5: C1CC(=CC)CCC1 SA 6: Nc1ccc(cc1C)C SA 7: NCCCN SA 8: O=S(=O)(OC) SA 9: c1ccc2OCOc2c1 SA 10: Nc1ncccc1 SA 11: N(CCCl)CCCl SA 12: c1cn(cnc1) SA 13: C=C(C=C)C SA 14: O=NNC SA 15: O=P(OC) SA 16: O(c1ccc(cc1)CC=C) SA 17: c1ncn(c1)C SA 18: C(CCCC(CC)Cl)Cl SA 19: c1ncsc1 SA 20: C=CCN SA 21: O=Cc1ccccc1O SA 22: O(c1ccc(cc1N))C SA 23: O1CC1C SA 24: SN(C)C SA 25: C(CCl)Cl SA 26: c1c(cc(cc1Cl)Cl)Cl SA 27: NNCC 4SA 28: O=CN(N) SA 29: C(OC)C(C)C SA 30: c1ccc2cc(ccc2c1) SA 31: Nc1cccc(c1C)C SA 32: NNc1ccccc1 SA 33: c1cc(ccc1C)Cl SA 34: N(CCO)CCO SA 35: Nc1ccc(cc1N) SA 36: c1ccc(cc1N)C SA 37: O(c1ccc(cc1)C)C SA 38: C(c1ccccc1)CO SA 39: C(=CCC)CC SA 40: N(Cc1ccccc1)C SA 41: Nc1ccc(cc1)C SA 42: Nc1ccccc1 SA 43: n1cccc(c1)
